name: Green Man
description: The Green Man is a legendary being primarily interpreted as a symbol of rebirth, representing the cycle of new growth that occurs every spring. He is most often depicted as a face surrounded by, or made from, leaves. Sometimes leaves may sprout from his mouth, nose, and other parts of the face. He can be found in architectural ornamentation, sculptures, and other artistic depictions, especially in churches and other religious buildings throughout Europe, dating back centuries, even pre-Christian times. The Green Man's origins are debated, with some suggesting connections to pagan deities associated with nature, fertility, and the wild. Others propose that he is a purely artistic and symbolic creation, representing the life force inherent in the natural world. While not a central figure in surviving Celtic myths, his presence reflects a deep connection to nature and the cyclical rhythms of life and death. He is often associated with figures like Cernunnos or other deities related to the forest. His enduring presence in art and folklore speaks to the power of nature's symbolism in human culture.
subtitle: Guardian of the Forest and Rebirth
appearance: The Green Man is typically depicted as a human face completely enveloped in foliage. The leaves may sprout from his face, forming a beard, hair, or eyebrows. Sometimes, vines or branches emerge from his mouth or eyes, signifying the flow of life force. The face itself can range from serene and benevolent to wild and untamed, reflecting the dual nature of nature itself. He may also appear as a full-bodied figure composed of plants, moss, and tree bark.
habitat: Forests, groves, ancient woodlands, and places where nature thrives. He is also commonly found in architectural carvings and sculptures.
behavior: The Green Man is generally seen as a benevolent guardian of nature, protecting forests and promoting growth. He can be capricious, rewarding those who respect nature and punishing those who harm it. He is a symbol of fertility and abundance, and his presence is often associated with positive change and renewal. He may appear to guide lost travelers or offer wisdom to those who seek it in the wilderness.

keyMyths.title: The Coming of Spring
keyMyths.description: The Green Man awakens from his winter slumber, signaling the return of spring and the renewal of life. His breath brings forth new growth, and his presence ensures a bountiful harvest.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of the Grove
keyMyths.description: The Green Man protects sacred groves from harm, ensuring that the balance of nature is maintained. He punishes those who desecrate the land and rewards those who respect it.

keyMyths.title: The Wild Hunt Connection
keyMyths.description: In some interpretations, the Green Man is associated with the Wild Hunt, leading a procession of spirits through the forest. This aspect highlights his connection to the untamed and primal forces of nature.

sources.author: Mike Harding
sources.work: A Little Book of the Green Man
sources.description: Explores the history and symbolism of the Green Man in art and folklore.
sources.text: Explores the history and symbolism of the Green Man in art and folklore.

sources.author: William Anderson
sources.work: Green Man: The Archetype of Our Oneness with the Earth
sources.description: Examines the Green Man as a symbol of humanity's connection to nature.
sources.text: Examines the Green Man as a symbol of humanity's connection to nature.
